qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth open redirect via URL encoding bypass in redirect validation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an open redirect vulnerability in @feathersjs/authentication-oauth via URL encoding bypass of a regex check. The advisory describes NoSQL injection via WebSocket id parameter in @feathersjs/mongodb where arbitrary JS objects pass through getObjectId() into MongoDB queries. These are different vulnerability classes (CWE-601 vs CWE-943), different components, and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: NoSQL injection via WebSocket id parameter in the MongoDB adapter where Socket.IO clients send arbitrary JS objects as the id argument, passing through getObjectId() into MongoDB queries as operators. The sole qualifying finding (oauth_open_redirect_url_encoding_bypass) describes an open redirect in @feathersjs/authentication-oauth, which is a different component, vulnerability class (CWE-601 vs CWE-943), and root cause. The closest finding is validated_symbol_bypass (non_qualifying_vuln) which describes NoSQL injection via VALIDATED symbol bypass in adapter-commons sanitizeQuery(), but this is a different root cause mechanism and was not classified as qualifying.
evidence: Advisory describes NoSQL injection via WebSocket id parameter in @feathersjs/mongodb (CWE-943)
Advisory root cause: Socket.IO transport layer performs no type checking on id argument, objects pass through getObjectId() into MongoDB query operators
oauth_open_redirect_url_encoding_bypass describes CWE-601 open redirect in @feathersjs/authentication-oauth - different component, different vulnerability class, different root cause

validated_symbol_bypass (non_qualifying_vuln) describes NoSQL injection via VALIDATED symbol bypass in sanitizeQuery() which is a different mechanism than the advisory-described getObjectId() id parameter injection
